body:
## Summary

Fixes #156.

Audits `link-calculator` as a published Rust library and expands its public
surface so downstream crates — concretely
[`formal-ai`](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai) — can drive the
calculator end-to-end through public APIs only:

- **Every artefact preserved.** New `Calculator::calculate_with_value` returns
  the parsed `Expression`, the structured `Value`, the per-step explanations,
  and the Links Notation re-encoding in one call. Nothing the WASM frontend
  shows is hidden from Rust consumers.
- **Primitives exposed.** `ExpressionParser::evaluate_with_steps`,
  `evaluate_expr`, `evaluate_expr_with_steps`, `apply_binary_op`,
  `evaluate_integrate`, `evaluate_at`, and `evaluate_expr_with_var` are
  promoted to `pub` with documentation explaining the downstream-consumer
  use case. `grammar::evaluate_power` is re-exported at the `grammar`
  module level.
- **Utils made public.** The `utils` module is promoted to `pub`, and
  `generate_issue_link` and `truncate` are re-exported at the crate root,
  so consumers can render the same issue links and apply the same
  truncation rules the calculator uses internally.
- **Parser borrow accessors.** `Calculator::parser` and
  `Calculator::parser_mut` let consumers reuse the parse cache and call
  into the lower-level primitives.

Crate publishability is preserved: `cargo package --no-verify --allow-dirty`
succeeds (415 files, 13.1 MiB).

## Requirements coverage

From the issue:

- [x] Double-check the library is exportable to crates.io with all
      functions and methods declared `pub`.
- [x] Ensure all calculation steps are reachable as Links Notation
      (including the final result), so no output is lost.
- [x] Make internal functions public where useful, so downstream crates can
      reconstruct calculations differently.
- [x] Provide an end-to-end example mirroring the downstream
      [`formal-ai`](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai)
      consumption pattern.
- [x] Deep case study at `docs/case-studies/issue-156/`.

## Verification

```bash
cargo fmt --check
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features          # clean
cargo test --all-features                          # 152 lib + 19 new tests + every other suite green
cargo test --test issue_156_library_surface_tests  # 19/19 outside-the-crate tests pass
cargo run --example library_consumer               # full round trip prints AST + LINO + steps + value
cargo package --no-verify --allow-dirty            # 415 files, 13.1 MiB
```

A changelog fragment under `changelog.d/` requests a `minor` bump so the
release pipeline cuts a new version on merge to `main`.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test --all-features` — all suites pass locally
- [x] `cargo test --test issue_156_library_surface_tests` — 19/19 pass
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` — no warnings
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` — clean
- [x] `cargo run --example library_consumer` — prints expected artefacts
- [x] `cargo package --no-verify --allow-dirty` — packages cleanly
- [ ] CI green on push (build, lint, tests across all jobs)
- [ ] Auto-release pipeline picks up the `minor` changelog fragment
title: Expand public library surface for downstream Rust consumers
